mission.title: kubeflow: jupyter-web-app: Set custom Image cmd and args
mission.description: jupyter-web-app: Set custom Image cmd and args. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current kubeflow deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kubeflow version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
helm list -n kubeflow 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kubeflow installation.

steps.title: Review kubeflow configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubeflow configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
kubectl get configmap -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubeflow
```
**Why you need this feature:**
Currently if someone would like to use a custom image they would need to create a Dockerfile, update the ENTRYPOINT and then push it to a public/private registry.

steps.title: Apply the fix for jupyter-web-app: Set custom Image cmd and args
steps.description:
#### What changes were proposed in this pull request?
This PR extends `PodDefault` to provide a capability to set a custom `command` and `args` for Notebook servers.

#### Example
- Create the following PodDefaut in the user's namespace:
```
apiVersion: "kubeflow.org/v1alpha1"
kind:
```yaml
apiVersion: "kubeflow.org/v1alpha1"
kind: PodDefault
metadata:
  name: custom-entrypoint
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      custom-entrypoint: "true"
  desc: "Lauch as Jupyter Lab"
  command:
  - jupyter
  args:
  - lab
  - --notebook-dir=/workspace
  - --ip=0.0.0.0
  - --no-browser
  - --allow-root
  - --port=8888
  - --NotebookApp.token=''
  - --NotebookApp.password=''
  - --NotebookApp.allow_origin='*'
  - --NotebookApp.base_url=$(NB_PREFIX)
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
kubectl get events -n kubeflow --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "jupyter-web-app: Set custom Image cmd and args" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
#### What changes were proposed in this pull request?
This PR extends `PodDefault` to provide a capability to set a custom `command` and `args` for Notebook servers.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: "kubeflow.org/v1alpha1"
kind: PodDefault
metadata:
  name: custom-entrypoint
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      custom-entrypoint: "true"
  desc: "Lauch as Jupyter Lab"
  command:
  - jupyter
  args:
  - lab
  - --notebook-dir=/workspace
  - --ip=0.0.0.0
  - --no-browser
  - --allow-root
  - --port=8888
  - --NotebookApp.token=''
  - --NotebookApp.password=''
  - --NotebookApp.allow_origin='*'
  - --NotebookApp.base_url=$(NB_PREFIX)

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubeflow installed or the issue environment reproducible.
